items.title: Wisma Putra confirms 59 M'sians safe following Mindanao quake
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Foreign Ministry has confirmed that all 59 Malaysian nationals registered with the Consulate General of Malaysia in Davao City, Philippines, are currently safe following the 7.8 magnitude earthquake that struck southern Mindanao at 7.37 am on Monday. The ministry also known as Wisma Putra in its media advisory said the Consulate General of Malaysia in Davao City had successfully established contact with Malaysian nationals residing in General Santos City, confirming that they are currently safe. "At present, there are 59 Malaysian nationals officially registered with the Consulate General in Davao City.

items.title: FT minister seeks end to Special OSC in Kuala Lumpur planning approvals over interference concerns
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The Federal Territories minister said she will no longer convene Kuala Lumpur’s Special One Stop Centre (OSC) and will seek Cabinet approval to abolish the body, saying it could create room for interference or the perception of interference in development decisions. In a statement today, Hannah Yeoh said the move forms part of the government’s broader governance reform agenda aimed at strengthening integrity and transparency in Kuala Lumpur’s urban planning system. The minister said Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) had already introduced measures aimed at improving transparency and accountability in planning approvals.

items.title: Sabah driving sustainable, digital tourism to guard against external shocks
items.summary: TUARAN: The Sabah government is blending sustainability and digital innovation to build a resilient, high-value tourism industry capable of mitigating external economic shocks. State Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor said such measures were taken as external factors could affect Sabah's tourism sector. Hajiji said he believed Sabah was on track to achieve its targeted four million visitor arrivals for Visit Malaysia 2026 and to prepare for Visit Sabah 2027.

items.title: WWF-Malaysia partners with Sabah coastal communities to protect sharks, rays
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: WWF-Malaysia is working with coastal communities in Sabah to conserve sharks and rays amid concerns over declining populations. In a survey conducted between November 2023 and December 2024, WWF-Malaysia and Universiti Malaysia Sabah counted, measured and recorded sharks and rays brought ashore at fish markets and landing sites across Kota Kinabalu and Sandakan. The research, approved and supported by the Sabah Fisheries Department, recorded 1,748 sharks and 2,890 rays representing 34 shark species and 45 ray species, respectively.

items.title: Music teacher in Klang pleads not guilty to 14 charges of sexual assault on six students
items.summary: KLANG, June 8 — A male teacher pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to 14 charges of committing physical sexual assault against six 15-year-old female students at a secondary school in Sabak Bernam. The accused, Idi Johan Mohd Zohdi, 45, a music teacher, entered his plea after all charges were read out before Judge Sharifah Hascindie Syed Omar. According to the charges, the man allegedly committed various acts of physical sexual assault against the six female students.

items.title: AirAsia introduces new procedures for specialised child restraint devices
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: AirAsia has introduced enhanced procedures for medically approved child restraint devices (CRDs) and submitted a new standard operating procedure (SOP) to the relevant authorities for approval. The airline said the review identified an opportunity to strengthen existing processes relating to the assessment of medically approved CRDs and other specialised travel requirements. AirAsia X chief safety and quality officer Captain Saravanan Subramaniam said the enhanced procedures were designed to better support guests with special needs while maintaining safety requirements.

items.title: Millions in prizes lure World Cup betting syndicates, punters
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: International gambling syndicates and local bookies are using lucrative cash prizes running into millions of ringgit to lure punters into placing bets for the 2026 World Cup, which kicks off this Thursday. With illegal football betting increasingly shifting to digital platforms, authorities are bracing for a surge in online gambling activity linked to the tournament, as syndicates exploit offshore websites, mobile applications and cross-border financial networks to evade detection. "Police anticipate an increase in illegal gambling and betting activities during the 2026 World Cup, particularly through online platforms.

items.title: Shrimp ban sparks tension as Malaysia demands answers, Thailand threatens WTO escalation over import curbs
items.summary: KUALA NERUS, June 8 — Malaysia is awaiting Thailand’s response to a questionnaire on shrimp imports before assessing the country’s compliance with local biosecurity requirements following the ongoing trade dispute involving the commodity. Adnan said Malaysia has also strengthened its biosecurity measures on imports of sea bass from Thailand following Bangkok’s decision to impose restrictions and conduct full inspections on Malaysian sea bass exports. “Our sea bass exports are also subjected to restrictions and comprehensive inspections by Thailand.

items.title: Court of Appeal upholds 36-year jail term for five over sword murder
items.summary: A three-judge panel comprising Datuk Azman Abdullah, Datuk Hayatul Akmal Abdul Aziz and Datuk Mohd Radzi Abdul Hamid unanimously dismissed the appellants' bid to overturn their convictions and sentences, which were handed down by the Kuala Terengganu High Court on May 9, 2024. The five men are Aizz Amidie Aziz, 32; brothers Mohd Rizalman Awang, 36, and Zahaman Fairol, 39; as well as brothers Mohd Uwais Mat Nawi, 31, and Mohd Hafify, 30. In delivering the judgment, Justice Azman said the appeals had no merit as the prosecution's witnesses, including a protected witness, had consistently testified that all the appellants were involved in stabbing the victim.

items.title: Ex‑Army chief’s wife’s jewellery seized under MACC Act, court warns assets forfeited if no claims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The Sessions Court issued a third-party notice today to anyone wishing to challenge the forfeiture of jewellery seized from Salwani Anuar @ Kamaruddin, wife of former Malaysian Army chief Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan. The third-party notice was issued under Section 41(2) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009 after the court allowed an application by deputy public prosecutor Mahadi Abdul Jumaat. Salwani was also charged at the Kuala Terengganu Sessions Court on January 26 with receiving RM5,000, also proceeds from illegal activities, allegedly deposited into her account at a bank in Jertih, near Besut, on January 16, 2025.

items.title: Nvidia unveils AI infrastructure deals in South Korea
items.summary: SEOUL: US chip titan Nvidia announced on Monday a raft of artificial intelligence deals in South Korea, where booming business for semiconductor companies is fuelling debate over how much of the profits go to workers. Nvidia, the world's most valuable company, unveiled with SK Telecom a "gigawatt-scale" AI data centre construction project, with the first facility set to come online in 2027. Nvidia also said it would work with chipmaker SK hynix to develop advanced memory components that are needed to run AI systems but are currently in short supply.

items.title: Penampang LPG cylinder fire: Man suffers 80pct burns
items.summary: PENAMPANG: A 29-year-old man suffered burns to about 80 per cent of his body after a liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) cylinder caught fire in the kitchen of an apartment unit in Taman Penampang today. Penampang Fire and Rescue Station chief Zeno Tingalan said firefighters received a distress call at 11.15am and dispatched six personnel, a fire engine and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit to the scene. "The team arrived at 11.22am and found an LPG cylinder on fire in the apartment's kitchen.

items.title: Balfour Beatty's US military housing unit exits government-mandated oversight
items.summary: NEW YORK: US federal government-mandated oversight over a Balfour Beatty unit has ended, the British construction group said on Monday, almost five years after the military housing subsidiary had pleaded guilty to defrauding the US military. To continue embedding improvements as part of the company's long-term operating model while maintaining focus on operational performance and the resident experience. Balfour Beatty Communities, which manages housing for US service members and their families, pleaded guilty in 2021 to fraud after falsifying maintenance records to boost company returns and agreed to pay over US$65 million in fines and restitution.

items.title: Entire civil service machinery to be mobilised against visa abuse - Chief secretary
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is mobilising its entire public service machinery to crack down on foreign nationals misusing visas, permits and licences for business activities in Malaysia, said Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar. The move, he said, follows Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's directive for firm, sweeping action to enforce the law and protect the nation's economic sovereignty. "I take the Prime Minister's directive seriously.

items.title: PH targets over 20 seats in NS election
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Negri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan is targeting to win more than 20 state seats in the upcoming state election to secure a clear majority. "The Negri Sembilan DAP committee meeting today discussed preparations for the Negri Sembilan state election after the state legislative assembly was dissolved last Friday. In the 2023 Negri Sembilan election, PH and BN formed the state government after controlling 31 of the 36 state seats.

items.title: Anwar to outline Malaysia's geopolitical vision at Nikkei conference in Japan
items.summary: TOKYO: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim is scheduled to deliver a keynote address at the 31st Nikkei International Conference, highlighting Malaysia's narrative in the global geopolitical landscape. Malaysia's ambassador to Japan Datuk Shahril Effendi Abd Ghany said Anwar's address, titled "Navigating Strategic Uncertainty", will focus on Malaysia's role in navigating geopolitical complexities while exploring new opportunities for regional prosperity. "The prime minister's keynote address will further underscore Malaysia's influence on the international stage, which always plays an active role in regional and global issues.

items.title: MOT yet to decide on diesel vehicle road tax cut
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Transport Ministry has yet to announce a decision on the proposed reduction in road tax rates for diesel-powered vehicles. Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said any decision on the matter would be subject to the outcome of discussions at the National Economic Action Council (NEAC). During the Road Transport Department's 80th anniversary celebration recently, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government was considering reducing road tax for diesel vehicles to cushion the impact of rising fuel costs.

items.title: Private jet market defies Iran war gloom as rich flock to Monaco GP, Cannes
items.summary: LONDON/PARIS/GDANSK: As soaring jet fuel prices triggered by the Iran war send ripples across the global travel market, a wealthy elite of CEOs, celebrities and sports stars is flying by private jet in greater numbers than ever before, to glitzy events from the Monaco Grand Prix to the Cannes film festival. Jet fuel costs have roughly doubled since the start of the war in late February, forcing global airlines to cancel flights and raise ticket prices, while missile and drone strikes around the Gulf have seen flights almost halve in a region that was a global connection hub. According to aviation data firm WINGX, the number of private flights has increased by about 4.0 per cent globally so far this year, adding thousands of trips.

items.title: PM Anwar heads to Japan to meet Takaichi, push new Malaysia‑Japan cooperation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim departed for Tokyo today to lead the Malaysian delegation on the Madani Diplomacy mission to strengthen strategic cooperation and national energy security, as the world faces a global energy supply crisis. In a Facebook post, Anwar said that during the visit, he will hold a meeting with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi to discuss the development of bilateral relations and to pioneer new Malaysia-Japan cooperation, particularly in the fields of economy, investment and energy security. Yesterday, the Foreign Ministry in a statement announced that Anwar’s official visit to Japan will begin today until June 10 at the invitation of the Japanese government.

items.title: More than 30 left homeless after fire destroys 10 homes in Kampung Cina
items.summary: KOTA BARU: An ordinary Monday afternoon turned into a nightmare for residents of Jalan Kampung Cina when a fire swept through 10 houses here today, leaving more than 30 people homeless. Fire and Rescue Department operations commander Wan Mohd Zain Wan Yusof said the department received an emergency call at 12.34pm and dispatched 20 firefighters from the Kota Baru and Kota Darul Naim fire stations to the scene. "Firefighters arrived at the scene five minutes later and found 10 houses engulfed in flames.

items.title: Police: Three‑year‑old girl injured in Sungai Petani crash reported stable, still receiving treatment
items.summary: The victim, Aulia Sofea Ahmad Shafiq, who sustained serious injuries, received treatment at Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital (HSAH) here before being transferred to Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital (HSB) in Alor Setar today. Kuala Muda district police chief ACP Hanyan Ramlan said she is still undergoing treatment. “The three-year-old girl is still receiving treatment.

items.title: Jaslinda loses nearly 10kg after 14-day ordeal, may go home this week
items.summary: TAPAH: Jaslinda Saludin, who survived 14 days alone in the wilderness near Gunung Batu Putih, is recovering well and is expected to be discharged from hospital this week after losing nearly 10kg during the ordeal. Tapah Hospital director Dr Wan Immi Salim said Jaslinda had shown significant improvement since being admitted on Friday night following her rescue. "If her recovery continues as expected, she may be discharged within this week," she said.

items.title: Sungai Petani crash: MOT awaits probe into accident that killed six
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The Transport Ministry is awaiting the outcome of a full investigation into a crash that claimed the lives of six family members in Sungai Petani yesterday. Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said the ministry would leave it to the relevant authorities to complete their investigation before commenting on the cause of the tragedy. He said the ministry was still waiting for a comprehensive report and would not speculate on any possible factors, including the condition of the newly purchased Proton X50 sport utility vehicle (SUV) involved in the crash.

items.title: Fahmi wants an AI prompt engineer in every digital community centre
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Communications Ministry aims to produce at least one prompt engineer at every National Information Dissemination Centre (Nadi) nationwide as part of efforts to strengthen artificial intelligence (AI) literacy and skills among young Malaysians. Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the initiative goes beyond exposing youths to AI tools, focusing instead on equipping them with the expertise to develop applications that can address local community needs. Present were Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar, Deputy Communications Minister Teo Nie Ching and Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah.

items.title: Tumpat court fines seven over attack on driving instructor linked to alleged derogatory remarks about student
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — Seven individuals, including a woman, were each fined RM2,000 by the Magistrates’ Court in Tumpat today after pleading guilty to a charge of rioting that left a driving instructor injured last month. Utusan Malaysia reported that the accused — Azli Mamat, 48; Mohd Azaki Mamat, 42; Mohd Khadri Ghani, 52; Ahmad Faizal Juhari, 35; Muhammad Alif Hakimi Abd Hamid, 22; Muhammad Izzat Romli, 25; and Nor Hasida Mamat, 38 — entered their pleas before Magistrate Wan Ashrul Afham Mohamad Azmi. Deputy public prosecutor Mahfuzah Hamizah Mohd Ariff prosecuted the case, while lawyer Shamsul Baharin Abdullah represented all seven accused.

items.title: New MACC chief orders all officers to declare assets within one month, pledges to lead by example
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner, Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman, has required all agency personnel to submit or update their asset declarations within one month of receiving an official notification, which will be issued at a later date. He said the directive was among the first measures introduced under his leadership to strengthen the agency’s integrity and rebuild public confidence in the MACC as an independent, professional and impartial institution. He also emphasised that the MACC would not yield to pressure from any source, whether public sentiment, media scrutiny or political influence.

items.title: PLKN to discuss voting arrangements if Johor, Negri polls clash
items.summary: PEKAN: The National Service Training Department will discuss with the Election Commission (EC) on suitable voting arrangements for trainees if the Johor or Negeri Sembilan elections coincide with the training programme. The third series of the National Service Training Programme (PLKN) 3.0 is scheduled to run from July 11 to Aug 24, while the EC is set to meet on June 12 to discuss key dates for the 16th Johor state election. Its director-general, Major-General Datuk Marzuki Mokhtar, said that if polling day falls during the training programme, the department will explore suitable options, including postal or early voting.

items.title: Teacher claims trial to 14 counts of sexually assaulting six students
items.summary: KLANG: A school teacher pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court today to 14 charges of sexual assault involving six of his 15-year-old female students from a secondary school in Sabak Bernam. Idi Johan Mohd Zohdi, 45, entered his pleas after the charges were read before judge Sharifah Hascindie Syed Omar. "I understand the charges.

items.title: Cruise demand rises as geopolitical tensions reshape travel choices
items.summary: Industry players said demand for cruise travel has strengthened as consumers look for itineraries that reduce exposure to airspace risks, flight rerouting, and border-related disruptions linked to global tensions. Against this backdrop, Singapore Airlines Group's loyalty ecosystem, through KrisFlyer and travel platform partner arrivia, has broadened access to cruise bookings using frequent flyer miles. The enhancement allows members to redeem KrisFlyer miles via KrisFlyer Cruise, arrivia's dedicated platform, across more than 30,000 itineraries operated by over 40 cruise lines.

items.title: MCMC warns against online gambling content ahead of World Cup
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) will act on complaints involving online gambling-related content ahead of the 2026 World Cup campaign. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said members of the public should lodge complaints with MCMC if they come across links or content promoting online gambling. Fahmi said similar action could also be taken against online gambling portals.

items.title: Gua Tempurung tower plugs coverage gap on North-South Expressway
items.summary: GOPENG: There are no stretches along the North-South Expressway (NSE) in Perak currently experiencing complete telecommunications coverage disruptions or blind spots, said State Communications, Multimedia and NGO Committee chairman Mohd Azlan Helmi. He said this was after a new telecommunications tower in the Gua Tempurung area began operations a month ago. Mohd Azlan said that apart from Gua Tempurung, the state government had previously addressed telecommunications coverage issues in the Menora Tunnel area.

items.title: Nancy Shukri says new care standards will safeguard dignity, rights across Malaysia’s care services
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The National Care Standards for Older Persons and Persons with Disabilities is expected to be launched in October this year as part of ongoing efforts to strengthen Malaysia’s care economy framework. Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said the standards are currently under development, following continuous discussions to establish clear benchmarks for quality care services, including expectations for providers within the care sector. “We are developing standards of care.

items.title: Telecoms boost in Perak as new tower resolves Gua Tempurung blind spot along PLUS highway
items.summary: GOPENG, June 8 — There are no stretches along the North-South Expressway (PLUS) in Perak currently experiencing complete telecommunications coverage disruptions or blind spots, said State Communications, Multimedia and NGO Committee chairman Mohd Azlan Helmi He said this was after a new telecommunications tower in the Gua Tempurung area began operations a month ago. Mohd Azlan said the Gua Tempurung area had previously been identified as one of the main locations experiencing telecommunications coverage disruptions, especially for users travelling through hilly stretches of road from the north and south. Mohd Azlan said that apart from Gua Tempurung, the state government had previously addressed telecommunications coverage issues in the Menora Tunnel area.

items.title: Bear roaming Japanese city forces closure of almost 100 schools
items.summary: TOKYO: A bear roaming the streets of a Japanese city for three days forced the closure of nearly 100 schools Monday, as dozens of hunters and officials searched for the animal. The city government of Utsunomiya, north of Tokyo, closed all 94 public primary and junior high schools in the area after receiving more than 10 reports of bear sightings since Saturday – including in a shopping arcade. In recent years, Japan has seen an increasing number of bear sightings and attacks, especially in urban areas.

items.title: ‘What is wrong is wrong’: Home minister says police ready to pursue Kamunting prison probe based on Suhakam findings
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — The government is prepared to undertake further investigations into the findings and conclusions of the public inquiry panel convened by the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia over last year’s incident involving the alleged ill-treatment of a newly admitted inmate at Kamunting Prison in Taiping, Perak. Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the Royal Malaysia Police is expected to carry out or complete investigations into the incident based on Suhakam’s report, which he recently presented to the Cabinet. On May 27, media reports said the Malaysian Prisons Department would fully cooperate with relevant authorities in reviewing the report and conclusions of Suhakam’s public inquiry panel into the alleged mistreatment of a new prisoner at Taiping Prison last year.

items.title: Johor Umno Youth: Loke 'confused' between national duty and politics
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor Umno Youth took a swipe at DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke, saying he is "confused between national responsibility and political choice" amid ongoing debate over cooperation between the two coalitions. Its youth chief Noor Azleen Ambros said the 'no DAP' stance reflected fundamental political divergence between the two parties and should not be mischaracterised as race-based politics. Noor Azleen, who backed Onn Hafiz's position, said the caretaker Johor menteri besar's stand was consistent with Umno and BN's state-level political principles, particularly the decision to rule out cooperation with DAP in the upcoming election.

items.title: No cover-up in Taiping prison death case, says Saifuddin
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: The government will not shield any wrongdoing uncovered in the death of an inmate at Taiping Prison last year, Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said. He said authorities must allow due process to take its course if investigations establish clear evidence of misconduct. He said the matter was recently discussed at Cabinet level following an inquiry report by the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam), which claimed multiple discrepancies and wrongdoing by the prison's management.

items.title: Six territorial army camps to host PLKN 4.0, train 10,520 recruits next year
items.summary: PEKAN: The National Service Training Programme (PLKN) 4.0 will undergo a major expansion next year, with 10,520 participants expected across four series compared with 4,200 recruits this year. National Service Training Department director-general Major-General Datuk Marzuki Mokhtar said the programme, currently conducted at three camps, would be expanded to six Territorial Army camps nationwide. "Series Two, which ended today, involved three camps, namely in Kuala Lumpur, Pekan and Malbatt Village in Negri Sembilan.

items.title: IT manager, assistant plead not guilty to 10 bribery charges linked to illegal Bitcoin mining raids in PJ
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, June 8 — An IT company manager and assistant pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here to 10 bribery charges for allegedly obtaining confidential information on raids against illegal Bitcoin mining. Lai Chung Lam, 40, and Lee Chee Yong, 43, pleaded not guilty before Judge Awang Kerisnada Awang Mahmud after all charges were read out to them jointly. The bribes were allegedly given in exchange for information regarding raids conducted by Tenaga Nasional Berhad (TNB) against premises suspected of carrying out illegal Bitcoin mining activities through electricity theft.

items.title: Police clarify younger brother was driving car in Sungai Petani crash
items.summary: SUNGAI PETANI: Police have clarified that the Proton X50 involved in yesterday's crash that killed six family members was driven by its owner, Ahmad Fahim Ahmad Shukri, 21, and not his elder brother as initially reported. Initial reports had named police corporal Ahmad Shafiq Ahmad Shukri, 29, as the driver. Adzli said Ahmad Fahim had only collected the newly purchased SUV yesterday, just hours before the fatal accident.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia ready to send aid after 7.9-magnitude Philippines quake [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia stands ready to assist the Philippines following a powerful 7.9-magnitude earthquake that struck off its southern region this morning. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim expressed deep concern over the disaster, saying Malaysia's thoughts and prayers were with the Filipino people. Malaysia stands ready to render whatever assistance may be required," he said in a Facebook post today.

items.title: Appeal court upholds 36-year jail term, whipping for five men in Besut sword-slaying case
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — The Court of Appeal today upheld the 36-year prison sentence and 12 strokes of the cane imposed on five men, including two sets of brothers, for the murder of a man who was slashed with a sword. A three-judge panel comprising Datuk Azman Abdullah, Datuk Hayatul Akmal Abdul Aziz and Datuk Mohd Radzi Abdul Hamid unanimously dismissed the appellants’ bid to overturn their convictions and sentences, which were handed down by the Kuala Terengganu High Court on May 9, 2024. The five men are Aizz Amidie Aziz, 32; brothers Mohd Rizalman Awang, 36, and Zahaman Fairol, 39; as well as brothers Mohd Uwais Mat Nawi, 31, and Mohd Hafify, 30.

items.title: Police correct earlier report, say SUV owner was driving in crash that killed six in Sungai Petani
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, June 8 — Police today confirmed that the Proton X50 sport utility vehicle (SUV) that collided with a lorry carrying a load of soil at Kampung Baru Pinang Tunggal, Sungai Petani, yesterday was driven by its owner, Ahmad Fahim Ahmad Shukri. Kedah police chief Datuk Adzli Abu Shah said there had been an error in the earlier information, which stated that the SUV was driven by Ahmad Syafiq Ahmad Shukri, 29, a Lance Corporal attached to the Seberang Perai Selatan district police headquarters. “Initially, the police were informed by the Fire and Rescue Department that the SUV was driven by a police officer.

items.title: 'Iskandar left me on my birthday,' says grieving father
items.summary: Ibrahim Ghazali, 39, said his son, Iskandar Affan Ibrahim, had spent his final day singing birthday songs and presenting him with a gift before the tragedy struck in Kampung Baru Pinang Tunggal here yesterday. Ibrahim said Iskandar had been especially cheerful and excited about riding in a newly purchased Proton X50 before the accident. Meanwhile, Iskandar's aunt, Siti Nur Atikah Ahmad Shukri, said she never imagined losing her mother, Nora Mohd Husin, 55; her brothers Ahmad Shafiq, 29, and Ahmad Fahim, 21; sister-in-law Jamaliah Sannusi, 29; nephew Ahmad Mikail Ahmad Shafiq, two months; and Iskandar in a single tragedy.

items.title: ‘Don’t burn bridges’: Fahmi, Dzulkefly caution against shutting doors in politics
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — Pakatan Harapan leaders today cautioned politicians against making overly rigid political declarations, amid continuing exchanges over caretaker Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi’s remarks that he would rather relinquish his post than sit at the same table as DAP. Speaking at a joint press conference after the Ministerial Dialogue and Media Roundtable on Safe and Responsible Suicide Reporting at the Putrajaya International Convention Centre (PICC), Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil appeared to urge restraint in political rhetoric. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dzulkefly Ahmad, who was seated alongside Fahmi at the press conference, said politicians should avoid closing off future possibilities.

items.title: Aaron says preserving historical records must be a shared responsibility
items.summary: IPOH, June 8 — The Community Archives Initiative has helped raise awareness among Malaysians that the preservation of historical records is a shared responsibility, said National Unity Minister Datuk Aaron Ago Dagang. Describing the initiative as a best practice, he said it facilitates the documentation of many valuable historical records still found in the community, including family albums, village collections, local associations, houses of worship, schools and personal collections. He said this to reporters after officiating the 30th South-east Asia and Pacific AudioVisual Archives Association (SEAPAVAA) Conference here today, which was also attended by National Archives of Malaysia director-general Zaidin Mohd Noor and SEAPAVAA president Karen Chan.

items.title: Malaysia can emerge as a leading rail nation in Asean, says deputy transport minister
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 8 — Malaysia is well-positioned to emerge as a leading rail nation in Asean through strong collaboration, strategic investments, technological advancement and continuous talent development. Deputy Transport Minister Datuk Hasbi Habibollah said the country’s rail agenda, guided by the National Transport Policy 2019-2030 and aligned with the aspirations of the 13th Malaysia Plan (RMK13), remains focused on building an efficient, integrated, sustainable and resilient transport ecosystem. “Rail plays a critical role in supporting economic growth, facilitating trade and logistics, improving urban mobility and connecting communities across the country,” he said when officiating the International Rail Transit Forum (IRTF) 2026 here today.

items.title: Penang reserves rise to RM616m, Chow Kon Yeow rejects deficit claims
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, June 8 — Penang Chief Minister Chow Kon Yeow today dismissed claims that the state’s reserves are depleting and that its finances are slipping into deficit. He said the state’s consolidated revenue account had increased to RM616 million in the first five months of this year. He said the state government is monitoring the situation closely.

items.title: Ahead of World Cup kick-off, Fahmi urges Malaysians to report online betting sites to MCMC
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today urged Malaysians to report online gambling content and betting websites to the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), warning that such activities remain illegal in the country. Speaking to reporters after the Ministerial Dialogue and Media Roundtable on Safe and Responsible Suicide Reporting at the Putrajaya International Convention Centre (PICC), Fahmi said members of the public play an important role in helping authorities curb the spread of online gambling platforms. “We know that online gambling is not allowed in our country.

items.title: ‘Think of the families’: Fahmi calls on platforms to act against graphic accident videos
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil today urged social media platforms to act more swiftly against the spread of graphic images and videos online, saying such content can cause further distress to victims’ families and potentially interfere with investigations. Speaking to reporters after the Ministerial Dialogue and Media Roundtable on Safe and Responsible Suicide Reporting at the Putrajaya International Convention Centre (PICC), Fahmi said the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) is looking to broaden its definition of online harm to include graphic or “gory” content. He said social media platforms should work more closely with authorities and immediately act to limit the circulation of such content.

items.title: Kesuma backs MACC investigation into alleged Daya Kerjaya 2.0 irregularities, says Ramanan
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The Ministry of Human Resources (Kesuma) has expressed full support for the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission’s (MACC) investigation into alleged irregularities involving claims under the Daya Kerjaya 2.0 Programme. Its minister, Datuk Seri Ramanan Ramakrishnan, said the ministry remained committed to ensuring all government assistance programmes are administered with the highest standards of integrity, accountability and good governance. He said the suspected irregularities were first detected through the Social Security Organisation’s (Perkeso) internal monitoring and anti-fraud mechanisms.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution: Refugee issue must be managed with balance between security and humanitarian concerns
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — The government will manage the refugee issue in the country in a balanced manner, taking into account both national security and humanitarian considerations, said Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail. He said an approach driven solely by emotion would not resolve the long-standing issue and stressed the need for wise and responsible management. We must consider the broader context and balance national security with humanitarian values,” he said when speaking at the Home Ministry’s monthly assembly here today.

items.title: Pakatan targets at least 20 seats in Negeri Sembilan state election, Anthony Loke to defend Chennah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) is targeting at least 20 seats in the upcoming Negeri Sembilan state election, DAP secretary-general Anthony Loke said today, as he confirmed he would defend his Chennah seat and lead the coalition’s campaign in the state. Loke said the target was based on PH’s performance in the 2018 general election, when the coalition won 20 of the 36 seats in the Negeri Sembilan legislative assembly. Therefore, we hope the people of Negeri Sembilan will give PH the mandate to continue governing the state,” he told Sinar Harian after chairing a special Negeri Sembilan DAP committee meeting here.

items.title: Penampang man suffers burns to 80pc of body in apartment fire involving LPG cylinder
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — A 29-year-old man suffered severe burns after a fire involving a liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) cylinder broke out in an apartment unit in Penampang, Sabah, today. According to Berita Harian , Penampang Fire and Rescue Station chief Assistant Fire Superintendent Zeno Tingalan said emergency services were alerted at 11.15am, prompting the deployment of six firefighters, a fire engine and an Emergency Medical Rescue Services (EMRS) unit to the scene. “One victim suffered burns to approximately 80 per cent of his body as a result of the incident.

items.title: From fuel to food, Hassan Marican warns crisis ripple effects will hit Malaysian households despite subsidy safeguards
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — The ongoing global supply and energy crisis should not be viewed solely as an oil price issue, but as a broader physical disruption that could gradually affect households through rising costs of living, according to Tan Sri Mohd Hassan Marican. The chairman of the Crisis Management Task Force of the National Economic Action Council said the impact would likely be felt in stages, beginning with fuel and logistics costs before spreading to petrochemical feedstocks, plastics, fertilisers, manufacturing and construction. Additional financing support for micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs) launched recently, he said, would also help businesses manage rising operating costs.

items.title: Jungle survivor Jaslinda Saludin smiles through fatigue, family says appetite and spirit returning fast
items.summary: TAPAH, June 8 — After enduring 14 days in the jungle, hiker Jaslinda Saludin is steadily regaining strength, with her family saying she is cheerful and has begun eating again. The New Straits Times (NST) reported that her sister Jasima, 52, said doctors are keeping a close watch on Jaslinda’s recovery. “Jaslinda is doing well.

items.title: Crisis task force chief says rationalised subsidy policy shields Malaysia, but soaring RM5b bill unsustainable
items.summary: Chairman of the Crisis Management Task Force (PPPK) Tan Sri Mohd Hassan Marican said the challenge lies in balancing immediate relief for households with the need to preserve national resources for the future. “Had the government not proactively pursued fuel subsidy reforms over the past two years, Malaysia would find itself in a far more precarious position today,” Mohd Hassan said in an interview with Bernama recently. Beyond ensuring sufficient fuel supplies, Mohd Hassan said the government is also strengthening measures to protect the broader supply chain that supports the economy and daily livelihoods.

items.title: Home minister: Cabinet orders immediate crackdown on foreigners conducting business on social visit passes
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — Home Minister Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the Cabinet has directed enforcement agencies to take immediate action against foreign nationals found conducting business activities while holding social visit passes. Saifuddin Nasution said all relevant agencies, including the Home Ministry, the Ministry of Housing and Local Government, and the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living, had been instructed to intensify enforcement efforts without delay. Saifuddin Nasution said enforcement action had already been carried out in several locations, including operations on Sabah’s east coast.

items.title: Saifuddin Nasution says Pakatan has settled all 56 Johor seats, approval now rests with presidential council
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, June 8 — Pakatan Harapan (PH) secretary-general Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail today said the coalition has finalised the allocation of all 56 state seats for the upcoming Johor state election following discussions at its secretariat meeting. Saifuddin Nasution explained that the decision still requires formal endorsement from the PH presidential council, as his role is limited to managing and facilitating seat negotiations. Saifuddin Nasution said the next step is to present the agreement to the PH presidential council, chaired by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, for formal approval.

items.title: MetMalaysia lifts tsunami advisory for Sabah’s east coast after powerful Mindanao earthquake, says no further updates expected
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, June 8 — MetMalaysia today lifted the tsunami advisory issued following a powerful earthquake off the southern Philippines, ending several hours of monitoring after authorities determined there was no continuing threat to Malaysia. In a statement issued at noon, the Meteorological Department said the advisory, which was issued at 8.36am, had been terminated. “The tsunami advisory issued at 8.36am on June 8, 2026, was terminated at 12.00pm on June 8, 2026,” it said.
